title: baphia pilosa subsp. batangensis
intro: Baphia pilosa subsp. batangensis is a plant species found in Malaysia and Indonesia, classified as a subspecies of Baphia pilosa, a legume in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid compounds. The health benefits of Baphia pilosa subsp. batangensis include treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed orally for its medicinal benefits.
